Help & Documentation

Welcome to the Blackjack Simulation tool. Below is an overview of the available parameters and the features you can use to analyze and improve your blackjack strategies.

Parameters

  • RL Method: Choose between QLearning, Sarsa, and BasicStrategy.
    • QLearning and Sarsa: Reinforcement learning methods that learn optimal strategies over time. They start with exploration and gradually exploit learned policies.
    • BasicStrategy: A fixed, non-learning strategy derived from classical blackjack heuristics.
  • Number of Decks (1-8): How many decks to use in the simulation. Increasing decks generally reduces count volatility.
  • Max Splits (1-3): The maximum number of times a pair can be split. Influences complexity and EV in advanced strategies.

Actions

  • Run Simulation: After adjusting parameters, run a bulk simulation of blackjack hands. The system will simulate up to 1000 shoes (configurable) and record results.
  • Generate Report: Downloads a comprehensive CSV file including:
    • Summary metrics (win/loss rates, EV per hand, final bankroll).
    • Hand-level records detailing each hand’s outcome, profit, actions taken, and dealer actions.
    • Shoe-level data, showing the complete card order dealt in each shoe.
  • Generate Strategy Chart: For RL methods, generates a heatmap-like chart (hit/stand decisions) reflecting the learned strategy for various player totals and dealer upcards.
  • Show Epsilon Chart: For QLearning or Sarsa, displays how the exploration rate (epsilon) changes over time, illustrating how the method transitions from exploring to exploiting the learned strategy.
  • Toggle Analytics Summary: Shows or hides an on-screen JSON summary of key performance metrics and distribution statistics, allowing quick reference without downloading a report.

Interpreting Results

Key metrics include:

  • win_rate / loss_rate / push_rate: Distribution of outcomes across the simulation.
  • EV_per_hand: Average profit or loss per hand, a crucial metric for long-term expectations.
  • final_bankroll / net_profit: Indicators of overall performance from the initial bankroll.
  • variance: A measure of result volatility. Higher variance means more swingy outcomes.

You can experiment with different RL methods, deck counts, and splitting rules to see how strategies evolve and what outcomes are sustainable long-term. Use the CSV reports for detailed, offline analysis.

For further guidance or troubleshooting, consult project documentation or reach out to the support team.
